title: See-Through Rectangles
description: Rectangles and opacity. Focus: Rect(left, top, width, height), width/height subtraction, and opacity (0–100).

flashcards.definition: Draws a rectangle. The first two numbers are the top-left corner (left, top). The third and fourth numbers are width and height.
Unlike Circle, a Rect uses its top-left corner (left, top), not centerX/centerY.
flashcards.example:
Circle(200, 200, 50)
Rect(200, 200, 100, 60)  # this rectangle starts at (200,200)

flashcards.definition: Width = rightX − leftX (y values don't matter for width).
Height = bottomY − topY (x values don't matter for height).
Opacity controls how solid/see-through a shape is. 100 = solid, 0 = invisible.

quiz.prompt: Which describes Rect(left, top, width, height)?
options: left/top is the top-left corner; width/height control size
left/top is the center; width/height are the radius
left/top is bottom-right corner; width/height are colors
quiz.feedbackCorrect: Correct: Rect uses the top-left corner plus width and height.
quiz.feedbackIncorrect: Rect uses the top-left corner (left, top) plus width and height.

quiz.prompt: What would the width be if left-top is (100, 50) and right-top is (275, 50)?
What would the height be if left-top is (100, 50) and left-bottom is (100, 150)?

options: Where the shape is on the canvas
How many points a star has
quiz.feedbackIncorrect: Opacity controls how see-through (transparent) a shape is.

quiz.prompt: Which opacity value makes a shape invisible?
